title: Before implementing, briefly analyze: which files need changes, potential breaking changes, edge cases. Then implement the task. Task: Review failed: review score 4/10 (min 7) issues to fix: - Then list up to 5 concrete fix items, one per line, using numbered or '-' bullets. - If score >= 7, do not...
summary_text: **Analysis** The change needed to stay in the verifier path: [assets/scripts/canon-v3-verifier.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/scripts/canon-v3-verifier.sh) plus its regression coverage in [assets/scripts/test-canon-v3-verifier.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/scripts/test-canon-v3-verifier.sh). The main break risk was over-filtering and dropping legitimate issue bullets, so I kept the parser strict only for known prompt-echo/meta lines. The edge cases I covered were lowercase `issues to fix:` headers, echoed instruction bullets, bare `codex`, and the “I’m checking…” process preamble leaking into queued fixes. **Implemented** I hardened the verifier’s issue extraction so prompt boilerplate and Codex preamble lines no longer become actionable fix items, and I tightened the review/recovery prompts to explicitly forbid role labels, process notes, and echoed instructions. I also added a regression case for the exact leaked output shape that triggered this failure. Verified with `bash assets/scripts/test-canon-v3-verifier.sh` from `/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config`.
